claims.claim: The target is openai/openai-agents-js at commit 710cccfd8fd26b395f8e3470419852d76de80967 under MIT.
claims.limitation: Applies only to the recorded commit and license bytes.

claims.claim: Five active candidates were scored and the selected example scored 100/100 under the precommitted rubric.
claims.limitation: GitHub activity and adoption counts are a dated snapshot.

claims.claim: Corpus v1.0.0 contains six synthetic-orchestration cases at the frozen SHA-256.
claims.command: npm run corpus:validate && npm run corpus:hash -- --check
claims.limitation: The corpus measures only applicable manager orchestration surfaces.

claims.claim: Canonical baseline-002 completed 4 pass, 2 fail, and 0 harness errors.
claims.limitation: This is not model-quality or production-incidence evidence. Two of the four passes (FR-004, FR-005) reproduce the example's own two shipped tests and were expected to pass; over the four cases designed by this teardown the baseline was 2 pass, 2 fail.

claims.claim: With all searches failed, the manager called the writer once and emitted a report.
claims.scope: FR-003 at the frozen manager boundary
claims.limitation: Does not show real-model fabrication or frequency.

claims.claim: After three negative verifier decisions and two revisions, the manager emitted the report.
claims.scope: FR-006 at the frozen manager boundary
claims.limitation: Does not establish verifier correctness or user harm.

claims.claim: The unchanged corpus passed 6/6 with the declared remediation patch.
claims.limitation: Proves only the tested manager control-flow oracles.

claims.claim: The remediation changes one upstream file and has SHA-256 129fc9c3fb37456fd9463ea856a7043a71df834f24b9f3e7ea1588ef17428997.
claims.limitation: The patch was not submitted upstream.

claims.claim: With the patch applied, the upstream focused tests passed 2/2 and the example TypeScript check passed after workspace dependency build.
claims.scope: local frozen checkout plus declared patch
claims.limitation: This is a focused compatibility check, not the complete upstream test suite.
